title: tanacetum coccineum subsp. chamaemelifolium
intro: Tanacetum coccineum subsp. chamaemelifolium, also known as pyrethrum, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. This plant is renowned for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel insects and ants due to its pyrethrin content, which is a natural insecticide. The health benefits of pyrethrum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Pyrethrum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in traditional medicine to create topical ointments and creams.
